So let's say, okay, fair it up, there's five elements that and if these five elements, if you can get someone really, really certain about these five things, if you can line them up at the highest level, right, you got like a 90 plus percent chance of closing that person, right? So it's like, it's like those five numbers to a common, if you know the safe combination, right? Well, you know, can you open it? Well, only if you're allowed to run them in the same order every time. It's not enough to know the five numbers of the combination or even three numbers of a combination. You can't open up the lock. If you don't run those in the right order. See, there's an order in which we go about closing someone and it works for everyone. Now, one of the beauties of the straight line certification is because when you're at this higher level of understanding, it allows you to essentially free form meaning that in the real world, I want you can't always follow a direct straight line pattern. You just can't. That's not rational. Doesn't always work. There are so many different situations out there. Like for instance, when you see me do that live sale and the milk, the fictitious sale, you notice I jump a little bit around. But if you'll notice, if you any two or three minute period, I end up in the same spot. I have these anchors. I always go back to my anchor spots and next thing you're like, wow, well, he lined up the elements in the exact same order. I might have to jump forward for a second to be elegant, but I always loop back because I know what my anchors are. Look at these five core elements of the straight line as your anchor points. These are the points that you have to hit at a certain point. Is there flexibility in the cost is flexibility. And the biggest problem I see people that don't apply the straight line well or the high level is their rigidness in its application. It must be applied flexible to be used in the real world. Right. And I explain what's going on. But the main takeaway is this difference between logical certainty and emotional certainty. Logical certainty is that sort of empirical boom, boom, boom, everything makes sense. Everything can add up. And let me just focus on one aspect. I really, I wish I hit this harder in the lesson, but it's only so long a lesson can be. And I also want to get to reinforce it later. Right. One of the most important aspects of being logically certain is that when you are, you can connect all the dots in your mind, you understand what gives something value, why it's why it's a great deal. And because of that, you can explain it to someone else. And you know, I made a joke about you get bitch like that was kidding. I know I was like, is that too politically incorrect? But hey, you know what the hell, the point I was trying to say is that if you, if someone is uncertain about their ability to say, well, why did I think this was a good deal? They'll tend not to make the decision because they know they're going to be possibly confronted with that by their partner or their spouse or their lawyer or their account. So they need to feel confident enough that they say, Oh, I bought it because here's the five. Here's why I bought it. Here's what makes it so awesome. You're going to be learning how to do that. At it's almost like, you know, the Navy SEALs, they train hard, fight easy. And there's a reason for that because there's a lot you got to learn before that. But when we get there, that is that insurance policy for logical certainty.
